headline: CONFIRMATION. Trial Day 3 (April 29) is procedural — Musk retook stand for cross-examination; Jared Birchall (Musk family-office manager / xAI / Neuralink executive) scheduled next; testimony covered Musk's $38M wire transfers to OpenAI and personal-fund use allegations. Day 2 (April 28) opening arguments crystallized two surviving claims: unjust enrichment + breach of charitable trust; remedy decision rests with Judge Gonzalez Rogers (jury advisory only); $134B-150B damages range; potential remedy includes Altman/Brockman removal. Verdict ~3 weeks out. Concurrently: PBC restructuring complete; OpenAI Foundation $130B equity stake; $25B philanthropic commitment announced. Illinois SB3444 committee deadline April 24 outcome remains unconfirmed in public sources today; Senator Cunningham's prior signaling that 'sweeping liability relief is highly unlikely' remains the directional read. Composite 27.5 (Developing) appropriately reflects unresolved governance controversy. Post-verdict re-queue mandatory after May 21 liability phase. No 5pt-threshold-crossing evidence in window.

evidence.finding: Trial Day 3: Musk retook stand for continued cross-examination; Birchall expected next. Testimony covered Musk's $38M wire transfers to OpenAI managed by Birchall's family office.
Day 2 testimony: Musk said OpenAI was created to counter Google/safety concerns; opposed for-profit majority value capture from charitable contributions.
Two surviving claims: unjust enrichment + breach of charitable trust. Damages sought ~$134B; remedy includes potential Altman/Brockman removal. Judge decides remedy; jury advisory.
PBC conversion complete; OpenAI Foundation holds ~$130B equity stake; $25B philanthropic commitment.

OpenAI testified April 9 in support of Illinois SB3444 — bill would shield AI firms from mass-casualty liability. Committee deadline was April 24; outcome unconfirmed in retrievable public sources today; Cunningham's earlier signal indicates sweeping form unlikely.

negativeSignals: Active trial featuring sworn testimony alleging breach of charitable trust + unjust enrichment is sustained negative INT/ACC pressure. Active SB3444 lobbying for liability-shield legislation is divergent from Anthropic's opposition position — sustained negative INT signal.
positiveSignals: PBC restructuring with $130B Foundation equity is a positive structural commitment. $25B philanthropic commitment is a positive ACT/INT signal. Trial transparency itself is a passive positive ACC signal.
post_verdict_requeue: Mandatory after May 21 liability phase + Judge Gonzalez Rogers remedy decision
